title: Conditional HTTP Fetching, Redirects, Compression, and Publisher Budgets · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in publisher-friendly conditional fetch client and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Store and send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses.
Classify 200, 304, redirect, temporary, permanent, auth, missing, rate-limit, and unexpected responses before XML admission.
Reapply scheme, authority, address, hop, credential, and loop policy at each redirect and record permanent moves separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy
steps.instruction:
Order the Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy Feed aggregation and Go source-to-reader-and-recovery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy, keep Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 5864 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove conditional HTTP, redirect and compression admission, cleanup, and publisher budgets; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply per-origin concurrency, minimum intervals, Retry-After, exponential backoff, jitter, identification, and operator override.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Feed Go Publisher Rate Policy has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
